title: tragia discolor f. lanceolata
intro: Tragia discolor f. lanceolata, also known as Lanceolate Spurge, is a plant species in the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and milky sap. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lanceolate Spurge are said to include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial activity. Lanceolate Spurge can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to support skin health and immune function.
